title: Who You Are in Christ — Part 1
intro: Your identity in Christ is not what you feel on your best day. It is not the sum of your successes or the weight of your failures. It is what God has declared to be true about you because of what Christ has done. And it does not change.
reading:
Gavin's outline lists seven aspects of who you are in Christ. Today we walk through the first four.

You are a child of God. John 1:12 says that to all who received Him, He gave the right to become children of God. Romans 8:15-17 adds that we have received the Spirit of adoption as sons — and if children, then heirs. You have been brought into the family. You are not a guest. You are not on probation. You are an heir.

You are a new creation — as we saw in Day 17. But more than that, you are righteous in Christ. 2 Corinthians 5:21 declares the great exchange: He who knew no sin became sin for us, that we might become the righteousness of God in Him. Your standing before God is not your own righteousness. It is His — given to you as a gift.

You are justified and forgiven. Romans 5:1 declares that since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God. Colossians 2:13-14 says God has forgiven us all our trespasses, canceling the record of debt that stood against us — nailed to the cross.

And Romans 8:1 — perhaps the most liberating declaration in all of Scripture — announces that there is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus. Not reduced condemnation. Not conditional condemnation. None. John 5:24 confirms it: whoever hears His word and believes has passed from death to life.
reflection: Which of these truths — child of God, righteous in Christ, justified, forgiven, free from condemnation — is hardest for you to personally receive? What is making it difficult to believe about yourself?
abide: There is no condemnation for me in Christ Jesus. I let that land today — not as a slogan, but as a verdict spoken by the only Judge whose word stands forever. I am forgiven. I am righteous. I am Yours.
